name: Shrimp Cantonese (Aka Shrimp in Lobster Sauce)
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Dry sherry or rice wine
1 tbsp Cornstarch dissolved in 2 tbsp Cold chicken broth
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat the egg white with cornstarch. Add shrimp, mix well, and refrigerate for at least 15 minutes or up to an hour.
2. Stir-fry the ground pork in 3 tablespoons vegetable oil just until done. Remove to a warming oven with a slotted spoon.
3. In the same oil (if it's not too oily), brown the pork. Add the remaining ingredients. Bring to a boil and add cornstarch-water mixture. Return to a boil and cook until thickened.
4. Turn off heat, then stir in the egg; fiddle with the mixture so the egg becomes thin strands rather than a large glob in the pan.
5. Stir in the cooked shrimp and toss with the sauce. Serve immediately.
You may substitute 1 1-1/2 lbs steamed, cut-up lobster for the shrimp to make Lobster Cantonese (or Lobster in lobster sauce). This will serve 2-3.
